epigraph.quote: The fire is not lit for the Griot. The fire is lit for the truth. The Griot is simply the one willing to stand in it.

blocks.text: INT. BLACK BOX THEATRE — NIGHT
An empty room. No seats arranged. No stage set. No lights beyond a single bulb swinging slowly above a bare wooden floor.
BROTHER JACK enters from the side. He does not perform his entrance. He simply arrives. Stands at the centre of the room. Looks at the space.
Then he looks at you.
BROTHER JACK
You are here.
a beat
Not because the industry told you to come. Not because someone promised you fame or money or a way out of wherever you are.
a long beat
You are here because something in you — something older than your reasons — decided this was necessary.
He walks slowly around the perimeter of the room. Looking at the walls. The floor. The ceiling.
BROTHER JACK
continuing
This curriculum has four phases.
Phase One is about who you are.
Phase Two is about what this body can do.
Phase Three is about how you serve the text.
Phase Four is about how you move through the world with what the first three phases built.
a beat
None of it is easy. All of it is necessary. And none of it works if you are here for the wrong reasons.
He stops. At the centre of the room. He draws a circle on the floor with chalk. Slow. Deliberate. Then a dot at its centre.
BROTHER JACK
Sit in the circle.
A beat. Then — quietly, with full weight:
BROTHER JACK
the instruction that begins everything
And begin.
INT. BLACK BOX THEATRE — CONTINUOUS
Brother Jack stands outside the circle. The student inside it. The distance between them — intentional.
BROTHER JACK
Every lesson in this curriculum has a requirement before you can move to the next.
Not a test. Not an exam.
a beat
A public act.
He walks to a board. Writes five words: Facebook. WhatsApp. YouTube. TikTok. Instagram.
BROTHER JACK
Open five accounts. One on each platform. Today. Before the next lesson begins.
Not because social media is the work. Because the work requires witnesses. And the Griot who stands in the firelight stands in public.
a beat
Each lesson will ask you to post. Something specific. Something honest. Something that costs you the comfort of keeping it private.
When you post — you include the lesson hashtag. That hashtag is the key that unlocks the next lesson. The system reads it. The system advances you.
a long beat
No post. No next lesson. Not because I am watching. Because the prior contract requires public commitment.
He erases the five words. Writes one: DAILY.
BROTHER JACK
Post every day. Not only for the lesson requirement. Every day.
Document the journey. The difficulty. The discovery. The days when it costs you something.
a beat
You are building a village before you know you need one.
Begin now.
Welcome to the Joelhood Acting Master Class.

What you are about to receive is not a collection of techniques. It is a formation. Complete, sequential, and cumulative. Each phase builds on the one before. Each lesson builds on the one before. You cannot skip. You cannot rush. The system will not let you.
That is not a restriction. That is a protection. Against the habit of consuming without being changed.
The requirement for this orientation lesson is simple: open your five accounts. Post about why you came. Not the polished version. The true one. Include the hashtag. And let the journey begin.
The Griot does not wait for perfect conditions. The Griot stands up. And begins.

requirement.prompt: Post about why you decided to begin this training. Not what you hope to get. Why you came. Include #JoelhoodOrientation.
